scope.summary: Implement reverse_seam_direction operation on Seam edges (flip the winding direction of a seam edge in the GarmentSpec, controlling which face is inside/outside after sewing)
acceptance_criteria: reverse_seam_direction(seam_id) flips the edge winding stored in tailor_seams and emits TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded
After reversal, the solver treats the opposite face as the outside face; cloth normals flip accordingly in the simulated mesh
SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH passes before and after reversal (direction change does not alter arc-length)
The gather_ratio sense (from_length / to_length) is preserved after direction flip
Model-accessible via edit_panel MCP tool with operation=reverse_seam
scope.risk_if_missed: Without seam direction control, garment panels can only be sewn with a single face orientation. Seams that need to turn under (collared necklines, set-in sleeves, waistbands) cannot be authored correctly. The garment will simulate with inverted normals on reversible panels, producing visual artifacts and incorrect collision response on those faces.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
